economic_notes: Duncan First Nation Development Corporation (100% owned), DFN Niyanin LP (99.99% owned), Wapiscanis Waneskwan Nipiy LP (150 units owned). Development Corporation had revenue of $1,492,373 and net income of $743,028 in 2024. Nation also operates a store selling confectionary and tobacco with $367,902 cost of goods sold.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED on two bases: (1) asset retirement obligations not estimated or recorded; (2) long-term investments (Duncan First Nation Development Corporation) were unaudited at time of release, and financial records for DFN Niyanin LP and Wapiscanis Waneskwan Nipiy LP were not complete. Wages and benefits total of $2,490,843 combines general wages/benefits ($2,229,873) and Chief and Council wages/benefits ($260,970). total_government_transfers includes WCTC ($10,028,406) + ISC Health ($1,611,143) + CMHC ($229,876) + Government of Alberta ($175,605) + First Nation Development Funds ($323,504) = $12,368,534 but resource revenue ($101,130) from federal trust funds treated as non-transfer; osr calculated as revenue minus government transfers. annual_debt_service reflects only long-term debt principal repayment of $52,450 (demand loan repayment of $214,929 excluded as it is a demand facility). No schedule of remuneration with individual amounts provided; only aggregate wages and benefits for Chief and Council of $260,970 disclosed in expenses by object. Prior period restatement applied to 2023 figures: accounts receivable reduced by $85,303 and deferred revenue reduced by $85,303. Assets under construction of $7,873,666 represent a significant portion of TCA additions.
warnings: Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
